title: Confluents
content:
As rivers seek the sea,
    Much more deep than they,
    So my soul seeks thee
    Far away:
    As running rivers moan
    On their course alone
    So I moan
    Left alone.

    As the delicate rose
    To the sun's sweet strength
    Doth herself unclose,
    Breadth and length:
    So spreads my heart to thee
    Unveiled utterly,
    I to thee
    Utterly.

    As morning dew exhales
    Sunwards pure and free,
    So my spirit fails
    After thee:
    As dew leaves not a trace
    On the green earth's face;
    I, no trace
    On thy face.

    Its goal the river knows,
    Dewdrops find a way,
    Sunlight cheers the rose
    In her day:
    Shall I, lone sorrow past,
    Find thee at the last?
    Sorrow past,
    Thee at last?
meaning: About: Christina Georgina Rossetti was a British poet, who wrote a variety of romantic, devotional, and children's poems. She is best known for her long poem Goblin Market, her love poem "Remember", and for her Christmas poem "In the Bleak Midwinter".
